titleStmt.title: Letter from J Williams to Sir Robert Chalmers, 15 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Williams

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Henry Williams, the secretary at the Office of Public Works, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). The Commissioners of Public Works acknowledge a certified copy of a letter of 12 September 1895 confirming the appointment of the harbour master at Dunmore East, County Waterford.Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/10145

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
OFFICE OF PUBLIC WORKS, DUBLIN 15 June, 1916. sir I am directed by the Commissioners of Public Works
to acknowledge with thanks the receipt of your letter
(9841) of the 14th instant enclosing a cortified copy of
the letter of the 12th September 1895 appointing Mr. Thomas
Sutcliffe at Harbour Master and Dunmore East, County Water- ford. I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient servant H.Williams
Secretary. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
